letters.label: Letter One
variants:
I fell asleep holding her hand.

I woke up holding nothing.

The seat beside me was still warm.

So I haven't been asleep long. So she can't have gone far.


That's what I keep telling myself.

Della

Philippa.

You fell asleep on my shoulder with the orange book still in your bag. I felt the corner of it against my arm.

Then the train hit a bump. Just one.


And you were gone, and the book was gone, and the warmth stayed.

I'm going to walk every carriage until it gives you back.

You can read this. I taught you that.

Della
